title: ERD Diagram Proof of Concept
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to see a quick ERD visualizing the entity relationships of the objects I have selected, so that I have a clear visual reference of how objects are related.
description:
## Context
Users need a visual Entity Relationship Diagram (ERD) to understand how selected Salesforce objects relate to each other.

## Behavior
The ERD is rendered using the GoJS library. Two PHP-generated arrays drive the diagram:

- **nodeDataArray** — one node per unique object
- **linkDataArray** — one link per foreign-key relationship between objects, built by iterating rows from `object_fields` where `data_type = 'reference'` or `field_api_name = 'Id'`

The source query:
```sql
SELECT id, object, reference_to, field_label
FROM object_fields
WHERE user_id = ? AND (data_type = 'reference' OR field_api_name = 'Id')
```

Links are created only when `reference_to` differs from `object` and the target object exists in the selected set. Each link carries cardinality markers (`0..N` / `1`).

## Implementation
- `entityRelationship.php` — static proof-of-concept with hardcoded data
- `entityRelationship_v2.php` — dynamic version pulling from the SQL query above
acceptance_criteria: ERD diagram renders using GoJS with nodes for each selected object
Links between objects represent foreign-key relationships from the object_fields table
Only relationships where both objects are in the selected set are displayed
Cardinality markers (0..N, 1) are shown on relationship lines
Diagram data is dynamically generated from the object_fields SQL query
ux_changes: New ERD visualization page rendering an interactive GoJS diagram showing entity nodes and relationship links with cardinality markers.
middleware_changes: PHP functions parse object_fields query results into GoJS-compatible nodeDataArray and linkDataArray structures.
implementation_notes: POC uses GoJS for rendering. Data is sourced from the object_fields table filtering on reference data types and Id fields. The linkDataArray builder checks that both source and target objects exist in the selected set before creating a link.
comment_summary: Developer requested a clarification meeting to understand how to make the static ERD dynamic, as the initial PHP file used hardcoded values.
